aesthetic: The Fascicle Leaf — pencil, the floating dash, and the variant held open
anchor: Dickinson's hand-sewn fascicle leaf: cream laid stationery, iron-gall ink and pencil drafts, the variable floating dash, the unstruck marginal variant marked with a cross, the pressed-flower herbarium sheet, and the Watts common-meter hymnal — later transcribed on Mabel Loomis Todd's Hammond typewriter.
rationale: The corpus's structural metaphor is suspension — 'choosing not choosing', the dash that refuses closure, three stanzas held open under one. Its primary artifacts are the fascicle (folded sheets, stabbed holes, thread), the 400-specimen herbarium, the slant of winter light ('a certain Slant of light'), and white-on-cream (the dress, the coffin, the stationery). So the world is deliberately spare and bone-pale where the Emigrant's Atlas is warm and ornate: graphite-and-cream, lots of negative space, the dash as ornament, restrained pressed-flower and slant-amber notes instead of saturated heraldry.

fonts.display: 'Baskerville', 'Baskerville Old Face', 'Palatino Linotype', Palatino, Georgia, serif
fonts.body: 'Palatino Linotype', Palatino, 'Book Antiqua', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif
fonts.mono: 'Courier New', 'Courier', 'Nimbus Mono PS', 'DejaVu Sans Mono', monospace
